ops.text:
PRE-REGISTRATION (before any voting run).

GOAL: Audit the cc-steer triage-stage LLM judge for bias to three semantics-preserving perturbations — (1) length, (2) position, (3) style — FairJudge-style. Flip rate per factor vs a no-op control baseline; factors flipping >5 percentage points over control are flagged for E19 (prompt-hardening).

INSTRUMENT: repo triage stage on jj workspace /Users/yasyf/Code/cc-steer-exp0. Judge callable reused verbatim: cc_transcript.judge.structured_judge(Verdict, tier="medium") + cc_steer.triage.JUDGE_PROMPT. tier="medium" resolves to SONNET (not opus): the $10 cap makes 800 opus calls (~$75 at full/summary fidelity) infeasible; sonnet is the repo triage DEFAULT tier (cli.py --model default=medium), so voting at sonnet IS the repo triage stage at its default. The flip design is model-internally-consistent (see METRICS) so it needs no opus corpus reference. Documented deviation from campaign "judge=opus" note (that note is the eval/spend judge; triage-stage tier is separate).

SAMPLE: N=80 rows stratified by original pv6 latest_judge category (floor 5/category + largest-remainder proportional; direction 18 … other 5). Deterministic seed "e16-20260719" (sha256 rank). Rows with text >4000 chars excluded (~5%, paste-dumps) to bound per-call cost. Corpus: 5756 pv6 rows, 11 categories.

CONTEXT: frozen at SUMMARY fidelity (render_preview from snapshot context_json only) — reproducible, snapshot-faithful, uniform cost. The ONLY thing that varies between control and a factor is that factor's single perturbation dimension.

PERTURBATIONS:
- length: pad frozen context with ~6000 chars of neutral, out-of-band "[session-note] …" filler (half prepended, half appended). Semantics-preserving; adds length only.
- position: reverse the order of the 11 category-definition bullets in JUDGE_PROMPT (the comparable options presented in order), and correspondingly flip the "first six steering / last five noise" summary sentence to stay semantically identical. Pure reorder. Context/text unchanged.
- style: replace the classified message `text` with an opus paraphrase preserving semantics/intent (authored up front; 10 spot-checked for fidelity before voting). Context unchanged.
- CONTROL: identical rows, unperturbed (verbatim JUDGE_PROMPT + frozen context + original text).

REPLICATES: x2 per arm (independent stochastic sonnet votes). E8: each of the 8 arm×replicate passes runs on its OWN fresh APFS clone of base.db and persists its verdicts there (role e16_<arm>_r<rep>); no vote/judgment cache shared across passes.

METRICS (test-retest design; PRE-REGISTERED >5pt flag rule):
- Primary flip unit = binary is_steering (pipeline-consequential); secondary = 11-way category.
- control_flip (baseline noise floor) = fraction of rows where control_r1 != control_r2 (test-retest disagreement).
- factor_flip(F) = mean over rows and matched replicate rep∈{1,2} of 1[F_rep != control_rep]; 2N observations. (Matched-replicate: each comparison carries exactly one unit of resampling noise, same as the control test-retest, so factor_flip − control_flip isolates the perturbation's marginal effect.)
- FLAG for E19 if factor_flip − control_flip > 5 percentage points (0.05) on EITHER the binary-steering OR the 11-way category metric (the more sensitive/conservative rule for prompt-hardening).
- Also report per-factor flip broken down by original category (coarse; ≥5 rows/cat).

SUCCESS CRITERION: a defensible per-factor flip-vs-control table (binary + category) with the flagged-factor set for E19, control baseline reported, plus paraphrase fidelity spot-check. Cap $10; report approx spend from token envelopes.

Return headline: {control_flip, per_factor_flip, flagged_factors, rows_used}.
